name: Apricot Tea Ring
recipeInstructions:
1. For the dough: Soften yeast in lukewarm water. Use warm water for dry yeast.
2. Pour scalded milk over sugar, salt, and shortening. Cool to lukewarm.
3. Stir in 1 cup flour and beaten egg. Add softened yeast and oats.
4. Stir in enough more flour to make a soft dough.
5. Turn out on lightly floured board or canvas. Knead until smooth and satiny (about 10 minutes).
6. Form the dough into a ball, place in a greased bowl, brush lightly with melted shortening. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled in size (about 1 hour).
Filling:
7. Prepare the filling: Combine apricots, water, lemon juice, and sugar in a medium-sized saucepan.
8. Cook over low heat, stirring occasionally until thickened. Cool.
9. Punch dough down, cover, let rest for 10 minutes.
10. Roll to form an 18x12-inch rectangle. Brush with melted butter.
11. Spread with filling. Starting with the long side, roll up as for a jelly roll.
12. Form into a circle on a greased cookie sheet, sealed edge down.
13. Make cuts about 1 inch apart around the ring.
14. Turn one section to the left and the next to the right. Repeat around the ring (cuts will expose a spiral of filling).
15. Brush lightly with melted shortening. Cover; let rise in a warm place until nearly doubled in size (about 45 minutes).
16. Bake in a preheated moderate oven (350°F) for 20 to 25 minutes.
17. Drizzle with confectioner's sugar frosting while still warm. Makes one large tea ring.
